title: When layering on top of a compressed image, podman build remove the layers compression and annotations
body:
When doing a derived build using an image with compressed layers as FROM, the resulting image layers are not compressed and are missing annotations.

For example Fedora CoreOS images have compressed layers: 
```"LayersData": [
        {
            "MIMEType": "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip",
            "Digest": "sha256:3ad966b00b5223a13ecd3d74ab11268e07ab7e837602c8b0ea11906d0d24f3b5",
            "Size": 1567491,
            "Annotations": null
        },
        {
            "MIMEType": "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip",
            "Digest": "sha256:aab5ce09aecad74a10ca3002622fbde68a1fd068fc08b79bd8035aaaf3a6b980",
            "Size": 40581841,
            "Annotations": {
                "ostree.components": "llvm18-libs"
            }
        },
....
```

An image built on top of it is no longer compressed, and is missing annotations:
```
 "LayersData": [
        {
            "MIMEType": "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar",
            "Digest": "sha256:f2640eec6f8a67ce478d464e2c83b48b2c86b15bf1f0fa87c1159e59dd8ad177",
            "Size": 11744256,
            "Annotations": null
        },
        {
            "MIMEType": "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar",
            "Digest": "sha256:921719229f2077d77dc4aa47938b2c670dcd91cd925702df6eb79adfd9798301",
            "Size": 118822912,
            "Annotations": null
        },
```

The man page says that `--compress` does nothing and is there for CLI compatibility.

IMHO, podman should not change the underlaying layers as it cause unecessary data duplication.

Simplest reproducer: 
Containerfile : 
```
FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos
RUN dnf install -y tmux
```

Compare the two images :
```
podman build . --from quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable --pull-always -t localhost/local-fcos-derive
skopeo  inspect containers-storage:localhost/local-fcos-derive:latest > layered-fcos 
skopeo  inspect docker://quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable --no-tags > fcos-stable 
```

Importing the image into local container storage does preserve the layers : 
```
podman pull quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable
skopeo  inspect containers-storage:quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable # same content as docker://quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable
```

# Version and info

comments.body: Could you please also add the version and output of `podman info`.

> Could you please also add the version and output of `podman info`.

Updated the initial post


I also did some more testing:
Pushing the layered image to quay does compress the layers again, thankfully ! But the resulting digests are different from the original image.
@jbtrystram Could you let me know what is actual issue which you are observing because of change in digests and compression ?

> @jbtrystram Could you let me know what is actual issue which you are observing because of change in digests and compression ?

This is an issue with native bootable containers (bootc / ostree).
For OCP we build the base image (rhel content) using rpm-ostree which results in an OCI image then use podman to layer the OCP bits on top.

The node boots the RHEL image then rebase to the layered image. 
The issue is that as the digests change, the node download the whole image even though, technically only one layer is needed.

Right, so concretely what's happening here is:
- when the base CoreOS image is built, [it gets chunked](https://github.com/coreos/coreos-assembler/blob/574b0e20067bf3bec146508414b9ce4ff6d26f77/src/cmd-build#L511) (and in the container-native path, this happens [here](https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-config/blob/dfe2cb8a63ba00a53237dc4cef33eb8f8bb9c924/Containerfile#L31)), which goes down the rpm-ostree -> ostree-rs-ext -> ocidir path, and the latter uses https://docs.rs/flate2/latest/flate2 to compress blobs in gzip
- when the node image is built on top of the CoreOS image and then pushed, those base layers get recomppressed using whatever particular gzip library c/storage (or I guess that'd be c/image maybe?) uses, which is very unlikely to be bit reproducible with the rpm-ostree path

I was chatting with @cgwalters about this and he mentioned that if we just pulled the base image first before building and pushing the node image, c/storage keeps track of the uncompressed <--> compressed mapping and so may reuse the compressed digests for the base layers when compressing the node image.

> I was chatting with @cgwalters about this and he mentioned that if we just pulled the base image first before building and pushing the node image, c/storage keeps track of the uncompressed <--> compressed mappin

I had to look (assisted by AI to find the code), I think it's https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/pkg/blobinfocache/boltdb/boltdb.go

> so may reuse the compressed digests for the base layers when compressing the node image.

But since this is doing a FROM of the base image (right?) the blob info cache should be populated by default; I think something more subtle is going on here, perhaps related to the `
FROM oci-archive:./out.ociarchive`.

Or really, the problem may be that the rpm-ostree chunking code needs to be able to query a c/storage instance for its cache and ensure that its layers are substituted with that cache from the start.

In the end really the goal overall is "the first gzip instance for a given layer wins".

OK cool, thanks to Colin's hint regarding the blob info cache, I was able to get this to behave how we want it.

The problem is that the base image is hosted on quay.io, but we push the node image to registry.ci.openshift.org. AIUI the cache is scoped to the target registry and so will not try to reuse the compressed digests from quay.io during the push. (Which seems reasonable; I guess the logic there is that c/image has no reason to think that the destination registry already has the blob.)

Fortunately, in our case, when pushing the _base image_ we pushed not only to quay.io but to registry.ci, so switching the `--from` when building the node image to use the one from registry.ci fixes it.

Another more general approach (which was my first solution) is to first `skopeo copy` the base image from the source registry to the target registry. Though that incurs more I/O since you're pulling layers twice now (during the copy and then again during the build) instead of once (during the build).

This is all quite subtle and hidden. Though I'm not sure of the best way to surface this to users. Perhaps something like `podman push --preserve-digests-from quay.io`?

> and so will not try to reuse the compressed digests from quay.io during the push. 

Offhand, the cost of an extra HTTP request here seems ~trivial vs the potential benefits of blob reuse so this seems like a bug/rfe for c/image.

> > and so will not try to reuse the compressed digests from quay.io during the push.
> 
> Offhand, the cost of an extra HTTP request here seems ~trivial vs the potential benefits of blob reuse so this seems like a bug/rfe for c/image.

AIUI, isn't the problem more fundamental than that in that the original compressed layer is not available anywhere? (Well, it's in the original registry, but it would be extremely awkward to have `podman push` to registry X actually also copy content from registry Y.) Again, this is my understanding, but I _think_ what may be happening in the "cache hit" path when pushing is that c/image references layer A, and the registry doesn't even ask for it to be uploaded because it already has it. But if it did, c/image wouldn't have anything to answer with.

I think a simple general fix is to pull the previous version of the target image before pushing, or more generally before pushing to *any* registry, pull from all target registries.

(This could be optimized to only pulling layers which have a corresponding diffid which is basically only pulling metadata!)

> 
> Fortunately, in our case, when pushing the _base image_ we pushed not only to quay.io but to registry.ci, so switching the `--from` when building the node image to use the one from registry.ci fixes it.

That's weird because I remember I tested that with FCOS images from quay and pushing again to quay.

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26088#issuecomment-2859367518

> That's weird because I remember I tested that with FCOS images from quay and pushing again to quay.
> 
> [#26088 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26088#issuecomment-2859367518)

Hmm weird:

>  Pushing the layered image to quay does compress the layers again, thankfully ! But the resulting digests are different from the original image.

I can't reproduce this.

```
$ cat Containerfile 
FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable
RUN touch /etc/foobar

$ podman build -t quay.io/jlebon/fedora-coreos:stable .                                                                                                                          
STEP 1/2: FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable                                                                                                                                                    
Trying to pull quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable...                                                                                                                                                 
Getting image source signatures                                                                                                                                                                       
Copying blob 941307076b86 done   
...
Writing manifest to image destination                                                                                                                                                                 
STEP 2/2: RUN touch /etc/foobar                                                                                                                                                                       
COMMIT quay.io/jlebon/fedora-coreos:stable                                                                                                                                                            
--> 711dfcfe528b                                                                                                                                                                                      
Successfully tagged quay.io/jlebon/fedora-coreos:stable                                                                                                                                               
711dfcfe528b2cd1e9c432a3871fa6bdbd6a9bce2d3859ce89d59b5c3aaa422a  
                                                                                                                                    
$ podman push quay.io/jlebon/fedora-coreos:stable
Getting image source signatures                                                                                                                                                                       
Copying blob 24a0429f6ef9 skipped: already exists
...
(lots of "already exists" here; as expected just one new blob, and the new config)
```

Then verifying that the layers are all the same except the last:

```
$ skopeo inspect -n docker://quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable | jq .Layers[] > base.txt                                                                                       
$ skopeo inspect -n docker://quay.io/jlebon/fedora-coreos:stable | jq .Layers[] > layered.txt                                                                                    
$ diff base.txt layered.txt                                                                                                                                                      
51a52                                                                                                                                                                                                 
> "sha256:f31592fd26dc3d061829e7d72c81ebdc068755071702423530e61c0326663674"
```

> I think a simple general fix is to pull the previous version of the target image before pushing, or more generally before pushing to _any_ registry, pull from all target registries.

This would help, but unless the base layers have not changed since the last build, I think all the new layers will still get recompressed and be different from the original base image. Over time and multiple builds, as more base layers get invalidated, less and less would be shared with the base image.

> This is all quite subtle and hidden. Though I'm not sure of the best way to surface this to users. Perhaps something like `podman push --preserve-digests-from quay.io`?

To explain this suggestion a bit more, the idea there is that users can tell podman to reuse the blob info cache from quay.io to be presented to the target registry, and if it doesn't know about them, just hard error. That at least allows users to enforce digest matching instead of having it be opportunistic. If they hit that error, users can the either drop the switch (accept that some layers will be recompressed), or do a `skopeo copy` before the push to populate the layers in the target registry.

Obviously, `podman push` could learn to do the equivalent `skopeo copy`, though that increases UX and code complexity quite a bit.

Is there a workaround for the issue of the layer annotations being lost? I'm running into what I think is this same issue where all layer annotations are lost when pushing a compressed image to a remote registry. I don't care about the layers being recompressed, but the `"ostree.components"` layer annotations are actually important (they're analyzed by [build-chunked-oci](https://coreos.github.io/rpm-ostree/build-chunked-oci/) to minimize changes to the layer chunking plan between image updates).
